description: monogate Challenge Board — open problems for EML/BEST symbolic regression

problems.name: sin(x)
problems.description: Express sin(x) as a finite real-valued EML tree with terminals {1, x}. The Infinite Zeros Barrier theorem rules this out for all finite N. Searching in the complex domain (terminals {1, x, ix, i}) is an open sub-problem.

best_known.node_description: 1-node EXL approximation — best real approximation by MCTS
best_known.complex_exact: Im(eml(i*x, 1)) — 1 node in complex EML domain

problems.hint: See complex_eval.sin_via_euler — exact in C, impossible in R. Proving impossibility for N≤11 is itself a publishable contribution.

problems.name: cos(x)
problems.description: Express cos(x) as a finite real-valued EML tree. Same barrier as sin(x).

problems.name: W₀(x)
problems.description: Express the principal branch of the Lambert W function in EML/BEST. W(x) satisfies W(x)·exp(W(x)) = x. Note: W(x) = x - x² + (3/2)x³ - ... The fixed-point iteration y → x·exp(-y) is directly EML-representable.
problems.hint: W(x) is the fixed point of y = x·exp(-y) = exp(eml(ln(x), y)). Unrolling this recurrence gives nested EML trees.

problems.name: erf(x)
problems.description: Express the error function erf(x) = (2/√π)∫₀ˣ exp(-t²) dt. The Maclaurin series is erf(x) = (2/√π)(x - x³/3 + x⁵/10 - ...). BEST routing reduces the polynomial node count significantly.
problems.hint: Start with the 4-term Maclaurin series, rewrite using BEST routing.

problems.name: Ai(x)
problems.description: The Airy function Ai(x) = (1/π)∫₀^∞ cos(t³/3 + xt) dt. Power series: Ai(x) = c₁·f(x) - c₂·g(x) where f(x) = Σ(3^k·k!/(3k)!)·x^(3k) and g(x) = Σ(3^k·k!/(3k+1)!)·x^(3k+1).
problems.hint: The oscillatory nature for x<0 suggests a complex-domain approach may be needed for exact representation.

problems.name: J₀(x)
problems.description: Bessel function of the first kind, order 0: J₀(x) = Σ (-1)^m/(m!)² · (x/2)^(2m). Like sin(x), J₀(x) has infinitely many real zeros, so the Infinite Zeros Barrier applies — exact representation in real EML is impossible. Best approximation challenge.

problems.name: Poisson PINN residual
problems.description: For the 1D Poisson equation u'' + f(x) = 0 with f(x) = π²·sin(πx), the PINN residual is L(u,x) = u_xx(x) + π²·sin(πx). Express the source term f(x) = π²·sin(πx) as a compact BEST tree for use as a PINN loss component.
problems.hint: π²≈9.8696. Express π²·sin(πx) = mul(π², sin(πx)). Use sin MCTS approximation as starting point.

problems.name: softplus(x) = ln(1 + eˣ)
problems.description: Express softplus(x) = ln(1+exp(x)) exactly in EML arithmetic. This is achievable! Find the minimal-node EML/BEST construction. Current best: 3 nodes with EML routing.
problems.hint: softplus(x) = ln(eˣ + 1) = eml(something useful...). Try working backwards from the EML gate.

problems.name: swish(x) = x·σ(x)
problems.description: Express the Swish activation x·sigmoid(x) = x/(1+e^{-x}) in minimal BEST nodes. Related to the known sigmoid construction.
problems.hint: swish(x) = x·sigmoid(x). Start with the sigmoid BEST construction, then add x multiplication.

problems.name: exp(-x²)
problems.description: Express exp(-x²) = e^(-x²). Note: eml(0, eml(x², 1)) = -ln(x²)... think about composition. Find the minimal-node exact BEST construction.
problems.hint: exp(-x²) = exp(-x·x). x² in EML costs 3 nodes. Then exp() costs 1 more. Total ~4 EML or ~3 BEST.
